summary: Cross-validated phasing order across IRQ1-IRQ9. Recommended order remains 004 -> 001 -> {002,003} -> 005, with Phase 004 guarded as render-layer plus invariant tests, Phase 002 P0 module resolution sourced from CodeNode.filePath, Phase 003 retaining no hard Phase 001 dependency for deterministic MVP, and Phase 005 bumped to L3 unless lifecycle/auth-cache hardening is split out.

title: Phase 002's file/module blocker does not add a new HLD dependency
recommendation: Keep Phase 002 dependent on Phase 001 only for architectural_role/classifyFileRole; source file/module rungs from CodeNode.filePath.

title: Phase 003 can remain dependency-free for deterministic MVP
recommendation: Do not add 001 to manual.depends_on for Phase 003 MVP; document layer fallback as unavailable/null when Phase 001 is absent.

title: TESTED_BY direction fix is Phase 003-local
recommendation: Use incoming TESTED_BY edges via queryEdgesTo across production symbols; no Phase 001 classifier knowledge is required.

title: Phase 004 still ships first only with render-layer guardrails
recommendation: Keep Phase 004 first, but require renderer-side uncertainty guard or producer invariant fixture, fallback hint behavior, and updated renderer/producer string tests; avoid scorer surgery.

title: Phase 005 should move to L3 if lifecycle/auth-cache hardening remains in scope
recommendation: Keep Phase 005 last and bump level to L3 unless subprocess lifecycle/auth/result-schema stress work is split into a prerequisite hardening packet.

title: Option D no-default enrichment does not couple Phase 003 to Phase 005
recommendation: Phase 003 ships deterministic output first; Phase 005 may evaluate enrichment only when explicit provider configuration exists.
